1	"""
2	Booking blueprint — all booking-related HTTP endpoints.
3	
4	URL layout (blueprint registered with no prefix so /schedule lives at root):
5	  GET  /schedule                                  – public schedule browser
6	  GET  /schedule/sessions/<id>                    – session detail
7	  GET  /booking/                                  – alias → my-bookings (backward compat)
8	  GET  /booking/my-bookings                       – user's booking dashboard
9	  GET  /booking/available-sessions                – HTMX: sessions for reschedule modal
10	  POST /booking/reserve                           – create reservation
11	  POST /booking/waitlist                          – join waitlist
12	  POST /booking/<reservation_id>/cancel           – cancel reservation
13	  POST /booking/<reservation_id>/reschedule       – reschedule reservation
14	  POST /booking/waitlist/<waitlist_id>/leave      – leave waitlist
15	
16	Business logic lives entirely in app/services/booking_service.py.
17	"""
18	import json
19	import logging
20	from datetime import datetime
21	
22	from flask import (
23	    Blueprint, render_template, request, redirect,
24	    url_for, flash, abort, make_response,
25	)
26	from flask_login import current_user
27	
28	from app.extensions import db
29	from app.models.studio import StudioSession, Reservation, Resource
30	from app.models.user import User
31	from app.services import booking_service
32	from app.utils.decorators import login_required, role_required
33	
34	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
35	
36	# No url_prefix — routes carry their full paths explicitly so /schedule lives
37	# at root while /booking/* routes live under /booking.
38	booking_bp = Blueprint("booking", __name__)
39	
40	
41	# ── helpers ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
42	
43	def _is_htmx() -> bool:
44	    return bool(request.headers.get("HX-Request"))
45	
46	
47	def _to_iso(date_mm_dd_yyyy: str) -> str:
48	    """Convert MM/DD/YYYY → YYYY-MM-DD (ISO) for HTML date input values."""
49	    try:
50	        return datetime.strptime(date_mm_dd_yyyy, "%m/%d/%Y").strftime("%Y-%m-%d")
51	    except ValueError:
52	        return ""
53	
54	
55	def _normalise_date(value: str) -> str | None:
56	    """Accept either MM/DD/YYYY or YYYY-MM-DD (from HTML date input) and return
57	    MM/DD/YYYY.  Returns None if the value cannot be parsed."""
58	    for fmt in ("%m/%d/%Y", "%Y-%m-%d"):
59	        try:
60	            return datetime.strptime(value, fmt).strftime("%m/%d/%Y")
61	        except ValueError:
62	            continue
63	    return None
64	
65	
66	def _error_fragment(message: str, code: int = 409):
67	    """Return a minimal inline error fragment for HTMX targets."""
68	    return render_template(
69	        "partials/booking/booking_error.html", message=message
70	    ), code
71	
72	
73	# ── Route 1 — schedule ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
74	
75	@booking_bp.route("/schedule")
76	@login_required
77	def schedule():
78	    """
79	    GET /schedule
80	
81	    Renders the class schedule for a given date.  Supports HTMX partial
82	    refresh: when the request carries an HX-Request header only the session
83	    list fragment is returned so the page does not reload on date navigation.
84	    """
85	    today = datetime.utcnow().strftime("%m/%d/%Y")
86	    raw_date = request.args.get("date", today)
87	
88	    # Accept both ISO (YYYY-MM-DD from HTML date input) and MM/DD/YYYY formats
89	    date_str = _normalise_date(raw_date) or today
90	
91	    instructor_id = request.args.get("instructor_id", type=int, default=None)
92	
93	    sessions = []
94	    try:
95	        sessions = booking_service.get_sessions_for_date(date_str, instructor_id)
96	    except ValueError as exc:
97	        logger.warning("schedule: bad date param '%s': %s", date_str, exc)
98	        flash(str(exc), "error")
99	        date_str = today
100	        sessions = booking_service.get_sessions_for_date(date_str)
101	
102	    # ISO version for the <input type="date"> value attribute
103	    selected_date_iso = _to_iso(date_str)
104	
105	    if _is_htmx():
106	        return render_template(
107	            "partials/booking/session_list.html",
108	            sessions=sessions,
109	            selected_date=date_str,
110	        )
111	
112	    instructors = User.query.filter_by(role="staff", is_active=True).order_by(
113	        User.username.asc()
114	    ).all()
115	
116	    return render_template(
117	        "booking/schedule.html",
118	        sessions=sessions,
119	        selected_date=date_str,
120	        selected_date_iso=selected_date_iso,
121	        instructors=instructors,
122	        selected_instructor_id=instructor_id,
123	    )
124	
125	
126	# ── Route 2 — session detail ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
127	
128	@booking_bp.route("/schedule/sessions/<int:session_id>")
129	@login_required
130	def session_detail(session_id: int):
131	    """
132	    GET /schedule/sessions/<session_id>
133	
134	    Full detail view for a single studio session.  Staff and admins also see
135	    the list of confirmed attendees.
136	    """
137	    session = StudioSession.query.get_or_404(session_id)
138	
139	    instructor = User.query.get(session.instructor_id) if session.instructor_id else None
140	    room = Resource.query.get(session.room_id) if session.room_id else None
141	
142	    # Deserialise equipment IDs and resolve to Resource objects
143	    try:
144	        equipment_ids = json.loads(session.equipment_ids or "[]")
145	    except (ValueError, TypeError):
146	        equipment_ids = []
147	    equipment = Resource.query.filter(Resource.id.in_(equipment_ids)).all() if equipment_ids else []
148	
149	    booked_count = Reservation.query.filter_by(
150	        session_id=session_id, status="confirmed"
151	    ).count()
152	
153	    # Check whether the current user already holds a reservation
154	    user_reservation = Reservation.query.filter_by(
155	        user_id=current_user.id, session_id=session_id, status="confirmed"
156	    ).first()
157	
158	    attendees = None
159	    if current_user.role in ("staff", "admin"):
160	        confirmed_reservations = Reservation.query.filter_by(
161	            session_id=session_id, status="confirmed"
162	        ).all()
163	        attendees = [r.user for r in confirmed_reservations if r.user]
164	
165	    logger.debug("session_detail: session_id=%d viewed by user=%d", session_id, current_user.id)
166	
167	    return render_template(
168	        "booking/session_detail.html",
169	        session=session,
170	        instructor=instructor,
171	        room=room,
172	        equipment=equipment,
173	        booked_count=booked_count,
174	        spots_remaining=max(0, session.capacity - booked_count),
175	        is_full=(booked_count >= session.capacity),
176	        user_reservation=user_reservation,
177	        attendees=attendees,
178	    )
179	
180	
181	# ── Route 3 — reserve ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
182	
183	@booking_bp.route("/booking/reserve", methods=["POST"])
184	@login_required
185	def reserve():
186	    """
187	    POST /booking/reserve
188	
189	    Creates a reservation for the current user.  Always returns an HTMX
190	    fragment — the form in the schedule/detail page uses hx-post.
191	    """
192	    session_id = request.form.get("session_id", type=int)
193	    if session_id is None:
194	        return _error_fragment("Missing session ID.", 400)
195	
196	    logger.info("reserve: user=%d session=%d", current_user.id, session_id)
197	    result = booking_service.create_reservation(current_user.id, session_id)
198	
199	    if result["success"]:
200	        return render_template(
201	            "partials/booking/booking_confirm.html", result=result
202	        ), 201
203	
204	    action = result.get("action", "none")
205	
206	    if action == "waitlist":
207	        return render_template(
208	            "partials/booking/waitlist_offer.html",
209	            session_id=session_id,
210	            message=result["reason"],
211	        ), 409
212	
213	    status_code = 403 if action == "blocked" else 409
214	    return _error_fragment(result["reason"], status_code)
215	
216	
217	# ── Route 4 — join waitlist ────────────────────────────────────────────────────
218	
219	@booking_bp.route("/booking/waitlist", methods=["POST"])
220	@login_required
221	def join_waitlist():
222	    """
223	    POST /booking/waitlist
224	
225	    Adds the current user to the waitlist for a full session.
226	    """
227	    session_id = request.form.get("session_id", type=int)
228	    if session_id is None:
229	        return _error_fragment("Missing session ID.", 400)
230	
231	    logger.info("join_waitlist: user=%d session=%d", current_user.id, session_id)
232	    result = booking_service.join_waitlist(current_user.id, session_id)
233	
234	    if result["success"]:
235	        return render_template(
236	            "partials/booking/waitlist_confirm.html",
237	            position=result["position"],
238	            message=result["message"],
239	        ), 201
240	
241	    return _error_fragment(result["reason"], 409)
242	
243	
244	# ── Route 5 — cancel ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
245	
246	@booking_bp.route("/booking/<int:reservation_id>/cancel", methods=["POST"])
247	@login_required
248	def cancel(reservation_id: int):
249	    """
250	    POST /booking/<reservation_id>/cancel
251	
252	    Cancels a reservation.  Returns an updated booking card fragment so HTMX
253	    can swap the card in place.
254	    """
255	    logger.info("cancel: reservation=%d user=%d", reservation_id, current_user.id)
256	    result = booking_service.cancel_reservation(reservation_id, current_user.id)
257	
258	    if result["success"]:
259	        reservation = Reservation.query.get(reservation_id)
260	        booking = booking_service.reservation_to_dict(reservation)
261	        return render_template(
262	            "partials/booking/booking_card.html",
263	            booking=booking,
264	            flash_message=result["message"],
265	        ), 200
266	
267	    status_code = 403 if "only cancel your own" in result["reason"] else 400
268	    return _error_fragment(result["reason"], status_code)
269	
270	
271	# ── Route 6 — reschedule ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
272	
273	@booking_bp.route("/booking/<int:reservation_id>/reschedule", methods=["POST"])
274	@login_required
275	def reschedule(reservation_id: int):
276	    """
277	    POST /booking/<reservation_id>/reschedule
278	
279	    Cancels the old reservation and books a new session in one operation.
280	    """
281	    new_session_id = request.form.get("new_session_id", type=int)
282	    if new_session_id is None:
283	        return _error_fragment("Missing new session ID.", 400)
284	
285	    logger.info(
286	        "reschedule: reservation=%d new_session=%d user=%d",
287	        reservation_id, new_session_id, current_user.id,
288	    )
289	    result = booking_service.reschedule_reservation(
290	        reservation_id, new_session_id, current_user.id
291	    )
292	
293	    if result["success"]:
294	        new_reservation = Reservation.query.get(result["new_reservation_id"])
295	        new_booking = booking_service.reservation_to_dict(new_reservation)
296	        resp = make_response(
297	            render_template(
298	                "partials/booking/booking_card.html",
299	                booking=new_booking,
300	                flash_message=result["message"],
301	            ),
302	            200,
303	        )
304	        # Tell HTMX to close the reschedule modal after swap
305	        resp.headers["HX-Trigger"] = "closeRescheduleModal"
306	        return resp
307	
308	    status_code = 403 if "only reschedule your own" in result["reason"] else 400
309	    return _error_fragment(result["reason"], status_code)
310	
311	
312	# ── Route 7 — my bookings (+ backward-compat alias) ───────────────────────────
313	
314	@booking_bp.route("/booking/")
315	@booking_bp.route("/booking/my-bookings")
316	@login_required
317	def my_bookings():
318	    """
319	    GET /booking/my-bookings   (also aliased at /booking/ for compatibility)
320	
321	    Renders the user's full booking dashboard grouped by lifecycle state.
322	    """
323	    all_bookings = booking_service.get_user_bookings(current_user.id)
324	    logger.debug("my_bookings: user=%d", current_user.id)
325	
326	    tab = request.args.get("tab", "upcoming")
327	    tab_map = {
328	        "upcoming": all_bookings["upcoming"] + all_bookings["pending"],
329	        "past": all_bookings["past"] + all_bookings["no_shows"],
330	        "cancelled": all_bookings["canceled"],
331	        "waitlisted": all_bookings["waitlist"],
332	    }
333	    bookings = tab_map.get(tab, tab_map["upcoming"])
334	
335	    template = (
336	        "partials/booking/bookings_tab.html"
337	        if request.headers.get("HX-Request")
338	        else "booking/my_bookings.html"
339	    )
340	    return render_template(
341	        template,
342	        bookings=bookings,
343	        active_tab=tab,
344	        upcoming_count=len(all_bookings["upcoming"] + all_bookings["pending"]),
345	        waitlisted_count=len(all_bookings["waitlist"]),
346	    )
347	
348	
349	# ── Route 8 — leave waitlist ───────────────────────────────────────────────────
350	
351	@booking_bp.route("/booking/waitlist/<int:waitlist_id>/leave", methods=["POST"])
352	@login_required
353	def leave_waitlist(waitlist_id: int):
354	    """
355	    POST /booking/waitlist/<waitlist_id>/leave
356	
357	    Removes the current user from the waitlist entry.
358	    """
359	    logger.info("leave_waitlist: waitlist_id=%d user=%d", waitlist_id, current_user.id)
360	    result = booking_service.leave_waitlist(waitlist_id, current_user.id)
361	
362	    if result["success"]:
363	        return render_template(
364	            "partials/booking/waitlist_removed.html",
365	            message=result["message"],
366	        ), 200
367	
368	    status_code = 403 if "only remove yourself" in result["reason"] else 400
369	    return _error_fragment(result["reason"], status_code)
370	
371	
372	# ── Route 9 — available sessions (reschedule modal) ───────────────────────────
373	
374	@booking_bp.route("/booking/available-sessions")
375	@login_required
376	def available_sessions():
377	    """
378	    GET /booking/available-sessions?date=MM/DD/YYYY&exclude_session_id=<id>
379	
380	    Returns a fragment listing non-full sessions for a given date, excluding
381	    the session currently being rescheduled from.  Used by the reschedule modal.
382	    """
383	    today = datetime.utcnow().strftime("%m/%d/%Y")
384	    raw_date = request.args.get("date", today)
385	    date_str = _normalise_date(raw_date) or today
386	
387	    # Booking card sends the reservation_id so we can look up which session to exclude
388	    reservation_id = request.args.get("reservation_id", type=int, default=None)
389	    exclude_session_id = None
390	    if reservation_id:
391	        res = Reservation.query.get(reservation_id)
392	        if res:
393	            exclude_session_id = res.session_id
394	
395	    available_sessions = []
396	    try:
397	        all_sessions = booking_service.get_sessions_for_date(date_str)
398	        available_sessions = [
399	            s for s in all_sessions
400	            if not s["is_full"] and s["id"] != exclude_session_id
401	        ]
402	    except ValueError as exc:
403	        logger.warning("available_sessions: bad date '%s': %s", date_str, exc)
404	
405	    return render_template(
406	        "partials/booking/available_sessions.html",
407	        available_sessions=available_sessions,
408	        selected_date=date_str,
409	        reservation_id=reservation_id,
410	    )
411

1	from functools import wraps
2	from flask import abort, request, jsonify, render_template, make_response, url_for
3	from flask_login import current_user
4	
5	
6	def login_required(f):
7	    """Redirect to login (or return 401 fragment for HTMX) if not authenticated."""
8	    @wraps(f)
9	    def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
10	        if not current_user.is_authenticated:
11	            if request.headers.get("HX-Request"):
12	                resp = make_response(
13	                    render_template("partials/error_fragment.html",
14	                                    code=401, message="You must be logged in."),
15	                    401,
16	                )
17	                resp.headers["HX-Redirect"] = url_for("auth.login")
18	                return resp
19	            abort(401)
20	        return f(*args, **kwargs)
21	    return decorated
22	
23	
24	def role_required(*roles):
25	    """Restrict a view to users with one of the given roles.
26	
27	    Returns an HTMX-swappable 403 fragment when the request has HX-Request header,
28	    otherwise aborts with 403.
29	    """
30	    def decorator(f):
31	        @wraps(f)
32	        def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
33	            if not current_user.is_authenticated:
34	                if request.headers.get("HX-Request"):
35	                    resp = make_response(
36	                        render_template("partials/error_fragment.html",
37	                                        code=401, message="You must be logged in."),
38	                        401,
39	                    )
40	                    resp.headers["HX-Redirect"] = url_for("auth.login")
41	                    return resp
42	                abort(401)
43	            if current_user.role not in roles:
44	                if request.headers.get("HX-Request"):
45	                    return render_template(
46	                        "partials/error_fragment.html",
47	                        code=403,
48	                        message="You do not have permission to perform this action.",
49	                    ), 403
50	                abort(403)
51	            return f(*args, **kwargs)
52	        return decorated
53	    return decorator
54	
55	
56	def htmx_required(f):
57	    """Return 400 if the request is not an HTMX request."""
58	    @wraps(f)
59	    def decorated(*args, **kwargs):
60	        if not request.headers.get("HX-Request"):
61	            if request.accept_mimetypes.accept_json:
62	                return jsonify(error="HTMX request required"), 400
63	            abort(400)
64	        return f(*args, **kwargs)
65	    return decorated
66

1	from datetime import datetime
2	from ..extensions import db
3	
4	
5	class AnalyticsEvent(db.Model):
6	    __tablename__ = "analytics_events"
7	
8	    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
9	    event_type = db.Column(db.String(50), nullable=False)
10	    # "page_view", "heartbeat", "booking_start", "booking_complete", "custom"
11	    user_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=True)
12	    session_id = db.Column(db.String(100), nullable=True)  # browser session token
13	    page = db.Column(db.String(500), nullable=True)
14	    data = db.Column(db.Text, default="{}")  # JSON
15	    ip_address = db.Column(db.String(45), nullable=True)
16	    user_agent = db.Column(db.String(500), nullable=True)
17	    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
18	
19	    user = db.relationship("User", back_populates="analytics_events")
20	
21	    def __repr__(self):
22	        return f"<AnalyticsEvent {self.event_type} @ {self.created_at}>"
23	
24	
25	class CreditHistory(db.Model):
26	    __tablename__ = "credit_history"
27	
28	    id = db.Column(db.Integer, primary_key=True)
29	    user_id = db.Column(db.Integer, db.ForeignKey("users.id"), nullable=False)
30	    event_type = db.Column(db.String(30), nullable=False)
31	    # "on_time", "late_cancel", "no_show", "dispute_upheld"
32	    points = db.Column(db.Integer, nullable=False)  # positive or negative
33	    reference_id = db.Column(db.Integer, nullable=True)  # reservation or appeal ID
34	    note = db.Column(db.Text, nullable=True)
35	    created_at = db.Column(db.DateTime, default=datetime.utcnow)
36	
37	    user = db.relationship("User", back_populates="credit_history")
38	
39	    def __repr__(self):
40	        sign = "+" if self.points >= 0 else ""
41	        return f"<CreditHistory user={self.user_id} {sign}{self.points} [{self.event_type}]>"
42

1	import os
2	import logging
3	from logging.handlers import RotatingFileHandler
4	from flask import Flask, session
5	from .config import config
6	from .extensions import db, login_manager, csrf, migrate
7	
8	
9	def _setup_logging(app: Flask) -> None:
10	    """Configure Python logging: console + rotating file handler."""
11	    log_dir = app.config.get("LOG_DIR", os.path.join(app.root_path, "..", "logs"))
12	    os.makedirs(log_dir, exist_ok=True)
13	    log_file = app.config.get("LOG_FILE", os.path.join(log_dir, "app.log"))
14	
15	    formatter = logging.Formatter(
16	        "[%(asctime)s] %(levelname)s in %(module)s: %(message)s",
17	        datefmt="%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S",
18	    )
19	
20	    # Console handler
21	    console_handler = logging.StreamHandler()
22	    console_handler.setLevel(logging.DEBUG if app.debug else logging.INFO)
23	    console_handler.setFormatter(formatter)
24	
25	    # Rotating file handler (10 MB, keep 5 backups)
26	    file_handler = RotatingFileHandler(log_file, maxBytes=10 * 1024 * 1024, backupCount=5)
27	    file_handler.setLevel(logging.INFO)
28	    file_handler.setFormatter(formatter)
29	
30	    app.logger.setLevel(logging.DEBUG if app.debug else logging.INFO)
31	    app.logger.addHandler(console_handler)
32	    app.logger.addHandler(file_handler)
33	
34	    # Also attach to root logger so SQLAlchemy / Werkzeug messages flow through
35	    root_logger = logging.getLogger()
36	    if not root_logger.handlers:
37	        root_logger.addHandler(console_handler)
38	
39	
40	def _seed_admin(app: Flask) -> None:
41	    """Create the default admin account on first run if it doesn't exist."""
42	    from .models.user import User
43	    from .services.auth_service import hash_password
44	
45	    with app.app_context():
46	        if not User.query.filter_by(username="admin").first():
47	            admin = User(
48	                username="admin",
49	                email="admin@studioops.local",
50	                password_hash=hash_password("Admin12345!"),
51	                role="admin",
52	            )
53	            db.session.add(admin)
54	            db.session.commit()
55	            app.logger.info("Default admin account created (username=admin).")
56	
57	
58	def create_app(config_name: str = None) -> Flask:
59	    if config_name is None:
60	        config_name = os.environ.get("FLASK_ENV", "default")
61	
62	    app = Flask(__name__, template_folder="templates", static_folder="static")
63	    app.config.from_object(config[config_name])
64	
65	    # Ensure runtime directories exist
66	    for folder in [
67	        app.config["UPLOAD_FOLDER"],
68	        os.path.join(app.root_path, "..", "backups"),
69	        os.path.join(app.root_path, "..", "logs"),
70	    ]:
71	        os.makedirs(folder, exist_ok=True)
72	
73	    # Logging
74	    _setup_logging(app)
75	
76	    # Extensions
77	    db.init_app(app)
78	    login_manager.init_app(app)
79	    csrf.init_app(app)
80	    migrate.init_app(app, db)
81	
82	    # Models (needed for migrate to discover them)
83	    from .models import user, studio, content, review, analytics, ops  # noqa: F401
84	
85	    # User loader for Flask-Login
86	    from .models.user import User
87	
88	    @login_manager.user_loader
89	    def load_user(user_id: str):
90	        return db.session.get(User, int(user_id))
91	
92	    # Refresh session on every request (sliding 8-hour window)
93	    @app.before_request
94	    def refresh_session():
95	        session.permanent = True
96	
97	    # Blueprints
98	    from .blueprints.auth import auth_bp
99	    from .blueprints.booking import booking_bp
100	    from .blueprints.staff import staff_bp
101	    from .blueprints.content import content_bp
102	    from .blueprints.reviews import reviews_bp
103	    from .blueprints.analytics import analytics_bp
104	    from .blueprints.admin import admin_bp
105	
106	    app.register_blueprint(auth_bp, url_prefix="/auth")
107	    # booking_bp carries explicit paths (/schedule, /booking/*) so no prefix
108	    app.register_blueprint(booking_bp)
109	    app.register_blueprint(staff_bp, url_prefix="/staff")
110	    app.register_blueprint(content_bp, url_prefix="/content")
111	    app.register_blueprint(reviews_bp, url_prefix="/reviews")
112	    app.register_blueprint(analytics_bp, url_prefix="/analytics")
113	    app.register_blueprint(admin_bp, url_prefix="/admin")
114	
115	    # Error handlers
116	    from .utils.errors import register_error_handlers
117	    register_error_handlers(app)
118	
119	    # Jinja globals
120	    from datetime import datetime as _dt
121	    import json as _json
122	    app.jinja_env.globals["now"] = _dt.utcnow
123	    app.jinja_env.filters["from_json"] = _json.loads
124	
125	    # Root redirect
126	    from flask import redirect, url_for
127	    from flask_login import current_user
128	
129	    @app.route("/")
130	    def index():
131	        if current_user.is_authenticated:
132	            return redirect(url_for("booking.schedule"))
133	        return redirect(url_for("auth.login"))
134	
135	    # Seed admin on first run (skipped during testing)
136	    if not app.testing:
137	        with app.app_context():
138	            db.create_all()
139	            _seed_admin(app)
140	
141	    return app
142

from flask import Blueprint, render_template, request
from flask_login import current_user

from app.extensions import db
from app.models.analytics import AnalyticsEvent
from app.utils.decorators import login_required, role_required

logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)

analytics_bp = Blueprint("analytics", __name__)


@analytics_bp.route("/")
@login_required
@role_required("staff", "admin")
def index():
    # Stub — implemented in Prompt 6
    return render_template("analytics/index.html")


# ── Heartbeat endpoint ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

@analytics_bp.route("/heartbeat", methods=["POST"])
def heartbeat():
    """POST /analytics/heartbeat — records a 15-second read heartbeat."""
    content_id = request.form.get("content_id", type=int)
    page = request.form.get("page", "")
    session_cookie = request.cookies.get("session", "")

    user_id = current_user.id if current_user.is_authenticated else None

    event = AnalyticsEvent(
        event_type="heartbeat",
        user_id=user_id,
        session_id=session_cookie[:100] if session_cookie else None,
        page=page[:500] if page else None,
        data=f'{{"content_id": {content_id}}}' if content_id else "{}",
        ip_address=request.remote_addr,
        user_agent=request.user_agent.string[:500] if request.user_agent else None,
    )
    db.session.add(event)
    db.session.commit()

    return "", 204
"}

1	"""
2	Content blueprint — all content-related HTTP endpoints.
3	
4	URL layout (blueprint registered at /content):
5	  GET  /content                           – public content browsing
6	  GET  /content/<id>                      – single content view
7	  GET  /content/editor                    – editor dashboard
8	  GET  /content/editor/new                – create new content form
9	  GET  /content/editor/<id>/edit          – edit existing content
10	  POST /content/editor/save               – save content (create or update)
11	  POST /content/<id>/submit-review        – submit for review
12	  POST /content/<id>/publish              – publish content (admin)
13	  POST /content/<id>/reject               – reject content (admin)
14	  GET  /content/<id>/history              – version history
15	  POST /content/<id>/rollback/<version_id>– rollback to version
16	  POST /content/preview                   – markdown live preview
17	  GET  /content/categories                – autocomplete endpoint
18	  GET  /content/filters                   – content filter management (admin)
19	  POST /content/filters                   – create filter (admin)
20	  POST /content/filters/<id>/toggle       – toggle filter active/inactive (admin)
21	  DELETE /content/filters/<id>            – delete filter (admin)
22	"""
23	import logging
24	
25	from flask import (
26	    Blueprint, render_template, request, redirect,
27	    url_for, flash, abort, make_response,
28	)
29	from flask_login import current_user
30	
31	from app.extensions import db
32	from app.models.content import Content, ContentFilter
33	from app.services import content_service, file_service
34	from app.utils.decorators import login_required, role_required
35	
36	logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
37	
38	content_bp = Blueprint("content", __name__)
39	
40	
41	def _is_htmx() -> bool:
42	    return bool(request.headers.get("HX-Request"))
43	
44	
45	def _error_fragment(message: str, code: int = 400):
46	    return render_template(
47	        "partials/error_fragment.html", code=code, message=message
48	    ), code
49	
50	
51	# ── index alias (used by base.html nav) ───────────────────────────────────────
52	
53	@content_bp.route("/index")
54	def index():
55	    """Alias used by base.html nav — redirects to public browse."""
56	    return redirect(url_for("content.browse"))
57

**`app/blueprints/content.py`** — 18 routes:
- Routes 1–13: browse, view, editor dashboard, new/edit forms, save (with file uploads), submit/publish/reject (HTMX badge swaps), history, rollback, markdown preview, categories autocomplete
- Routes 15–18: filter CRUD (list, create, toggle, delete)
- `index` alias at `/content/index` → redirects to browse (preserves `url_for('content.index')` compatibility)

**`app/blueprints/analytics.py`** — heartbeat route added at `POST /analytics/heartbeat` (204 response, records `AnalyticsEvent`)

**Templates created (14 files):**

|---|---|

| `partials/content/save_error.html` | Error fragment with violations list |
| `partials/content/category_options.html` | Datalist `<option>` fragments |

━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━

from app.services import content_service, file_service
from app.services.content_filter_service import filter_content
from app.models.content import Content, ContentVersion, ContentAttachment, ContentFilter

def test_create_draft(app, db, editor_user):
    """Content saved with status 'draft'."""
    with app.app_context():
        result = content_service.save_content(None, {
            "title": "New Article",
            "content_type": "article",
            "body": "Some body text here",
            "body_format": "markdown",
            "status": "draft"
        }, editor_user.id)
        assert result["success"] is True
        content = Content.query.get(result["content_id"])
        assert content.status == "draft"
        assert content.title == "New Article"

def test_create_content_creates_version(app, db, editor_user):
    """Creating content also creates a version 1 record."""
    with app.app_context():
        result = content_service.save_content(None, {
            "title": "Versioned Article",
            "content_type": "article",
            "body": "Body",
            "body_format": "markdown",
            "status": "draft"
        }, editor_user.id)
        versions = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(content_id=result["content_id"]).all()
        assert len(versions) == 1
        assert versions[0].version_number == 1

def test_update_content_creates_snapshot(app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
    """Updating content creates a version snapshot of the previous state."""
    with app.app_context():
        old_body = sample_content.body
        result = content_service.save_content(sample_content.id, {
            "title": "Updated Title",
            "content_type": "article",
            "body": "Updated body text",
            "body_format": "markdown",
            "status": "draft"
        }, editor_user.id)
        assert result["success"] is True
        versions = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(content_id=sample_content.id).order_by(ContentVersion.version_number.desc()).all()
        assert len(versions) >= 2
        assert versions[-1].body == old_body  # Original body preserved in version

def test_submit_for_review(app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
    """Status changes from draft to in_review."""
    with app.app_context():
        result = content_service.submit_for_review(sample_content.id, editor_user.id)
        assert result["success"] is True
        content = Content.query.get(sample_content.id)
        assert content.status == "in_review"

def test_submit_non_draft_fails(app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
    """Cannot submit already in-review content."""
    with app.app_context():
        sample_content.status = "in_review"
        db.session.commit()
        result = content_service.submit_for_review(sample_content.id, editor_user.id)
        assert result["success"] is False

def test_publish_creates_version(app, db, sample_users, editor_user, sample_content):
    """Publishing creates a new ContentVersion with status 'published'."""
    with app.app_context():
        sample_content.status = "in_review"
        db.session.commit()
        result = content_service.publish_content(sample_content.id, sample_users["admin"].id)
        assert result["success"] is True
        content = Content.query.get(sample_content.id)
        assert content.status == "published"
        assert content.published_at is not None
        versions = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(content_id=sample_content.id).all()
        assert any(v.status == "published" for v in versions)

def test_publish_requires_admin(app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
    """Non-admin cannot publish."""
    with app.app_context():
        sample_content.status = "in_review"
        db.session.commit()
        result = content_service.publish_content(sample_content.id, editor_user.id)
        assert result["success"] is False
        assert "admin" in result["reason"].lower()

def test_reject_requires_note(app, db, sample_users, sample_content):
    """Rejection without note fails."""
    with app.app_context():
        sample_content.status = "in_review"
        db.session.commit()
        result = content_service.reject_content(sample_content.id, sample_users["admin"].id, "")
        assert result["success"] is False

def test_rollback_restores_body(app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
    """Rollback restores body to selected version."""
    with app.app_context():
        original_body = sample_content.body
        content_service.save_content(sample_content.id, {
            "title": sample_content.title,
            "content_type": "article",
            "body": "Completely different body",
            "body_format": "markdown",
            "status": "draft"
        }, editor_user.id)
        assert Content.query.get(sample_content.id).body == "Completely different body"
        version1 = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(content_id=sample_content.id, version_number=1).first()
        result = content_service.rollback_to_version(sample_content.id, version1.id, editor_user.id)
        assert result["success"] is True
        assert Content.query.get(sample_content.id).body == original_body
        assert Content.query.get(sample_content.id).status == "draft"

def test_content_filter_keyword(app, db, editor_user, sample_filter):
    """Content containing a filtered keyword is blocked."""
    with app.app_context():
        result = content_service.save_content(None, {
            "title": "Article with badword inside",
            "content_type": "article",
            "body": "Normal body",
            "body_format": "markdown",
            "status": "draft"
        }, editor_user.id)
        assert result["success"] is False
        assert len(result["violations"]) > 0

def test_content_filter_regex(app, db):
    """Regex filter catches matching patterns."""
    with app.app_context():
        regex_filter = ContentFilter(pattern=r"\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b", filter_type="regex", is_active=True)
        db.session.add(regex_filter)
        db.session.commit()
        result = filter_content("My SSN is 123-45-6789 please help")
        assert result["passed"] is False
        assert len(result["violations"]) == 1

def test_content_filter_inactive_ignored(app, db):
    """Inactive filters are not applied."""
    with app.app_context():
        f = ContentFilter(pattern="testword", filter_type="keyword", is_active=False)
        db.session.add(f)
        db.session.commit()
        result = filter_content("This has testword in it")
        assert result["passed"] is True

def test_file_upload_validates_type(app, db, sample_content):
    """Executable file is rejected, jpg accepted."""
    with app.app_context():
        from io import BytesIO
        from werkzeug.datastructures import FileStorage
        bad_file = FileStorage(stream=BytesIO(b"fake data"), filename="hack.exe")
        result = file_service.upload_file(bad_file, sample_content.id)
        assert result["success"] is False
        assert ".exe" in result["reason"]

def test_file_upload_validates_size(app, db, sample_content):
    """File over 10MB is rejected."""
    with app.app_context():
        from io import BytesIO
        from werkzeug.datastructures import FileStorage
        large_data = b"x" * (11 * 1024 * 1024)
        big_file = FileStorage(stream=BytesIO(large_data), filename="big.jpg")
        result = file_service.upload_file(big_file, sample_content.id)
        assert result["success"] is False
        assert "10 MB" in result["reason"]

def test_duplicate_file_rejected(app, db, sample_content):
    """Same file uploaded twice is rejected by fingerprint."""
    with app.app_context():
        from io import BytesIO
        from werkzeug.datastructures import FileStorage
        data = b"unique test content for dedup"
        file1 = FileStorage(stream=BytesIO(data), filename="photo1.jpg")
        result1 = file_service.upload_file(file1, sample_content.id)
        assert result1["success"] is True
        file2 = FileStorage(stream=BytesIO(data), filename="photo2.jpg")
        result2 = file_service.upload_file(file2, sample_content.id)
        assert result2["success"] is False
        assert "already been uploaded" in result2["reason"]

def test_markdown_preview(app):
    """Markdown is converted to HTML."""
    with app.app_context():
        html = content_service.preview_markdown("# Hello\n**Bold text**")
        assert "<h1>" in html or "<h1" in html
        assert "<strong>" in html

def test_editor_dashboard_editors_see_own(app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
    """Editors see only their own content."""
    with app.app_context():
        items = content_service.get_editor_dashboard(editor_user.id, "editor")
        assert len(items) >= 1
        assert all(item["author_name"] == editor_user.username for item in items)

        content_id=content.id,
        version_number=1,
        title=content.title,
        body=content.body,
        status="draft",
        created_by=editor_user.id,
        change_note="Initial creation",
    )
    db.session.add(version)
    db.session.commit()
    return content

{"file_path": "/home/leul/Documents/task-7/repo/studioops/unit_tests/test_content.py", "content": """"
Unit tests for content management business logic.
Covers: content_service, file_service, content_filter_service.
"""
import pytest
from io import BytesIO

from werkzeug.datastructures import FileStorage

from app.extensions import db as _db
from app.models.content import Content, ContentVersion, ContentAttachment, ContentFilter
from app.services import content_service, file_service
from app.services.content_filter_service import filter_content


# ── test_create_draft ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestCreateDraft:
    def test_create_draft(self, app, db, editor_user):
        """Content saved with status 'draft'."""
        with app.app_context():
            result = content_service.save_content(None, {
                "title": "New Article",
                "content_type": "article",
                "body": "Some body text here",
                "body_format": "markdown",
                "status": "draft",
            }, editor_user.id)
            assert result["success"] is True
            content = Content.query.get(result["content_id"])
            assert content.status == "draft"
            assert content.title == "New Article"

    def test_create_sets_author(self, app, db, editor_user):
        """Created content is owned by the submitting author."""
        with app.app_context():
            result = content_service.save_content(None, {
                "title": "Authored Article",
                "content_type": "article",
                "body": "Body",
                "body_format": "markdown",
                "status": "draft",
            }, editor_user.id)
            content = Content.query.get(result["content_id"])
            assert content.author_id == editor_user.id

    def test_title_required(self, app, db, editor_user):
        """Empty title is rejected."""
        with app.app_context():
            result = content_service.save_content(None, {
                "title": "   ",
                "content_type": "article",
                "body": "Body",
                "body_format": "markdown",
                "status": "draft",
            }, editor_user.id)
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert "required" in result["reason"].lower()

    def test_title_max_length(self, app, db, editor_user):
        """Title exceeding 300 characters is rejected."""
        with app.app_context():
            result = content_service.save_content(None, {
                "title": "x" * 301,
                "content_type": "article",
                "body": "Body",
                "body_format": "markdown",
                "status": "draft",
            }, editor_user.id)
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert "300" in result["reason"]


# ── test_version_tracking ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestVersionTracking:
    def test_create_content_creates_version(self, app, db, editor_user):
        """Creating content also creates a version 1 record."""
        with app.app_context():
            result = content_service.save_content(None, {
                "title": "Versioned Article",
                "content_type": "article",
                "body": "Body",
                "body_format": "markdown",
                "status": "draft",
            }, editor_user.id)
            versions = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(content_id=result["content_id"]).all()
            assert len(versions) == 1
            assert versions[0].version_number == 1
            assert versions[0].change_note == "Initial creation"

    def test_update_content_creates_snapshot(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Updating content creates a version snapshot of the previous state."""
        with app.app_context():
            old_body = sample_content.body
            result = content_service.save_content(sample_content.id, {
                "title": "Updated Title",
                "content_type": "article",
                "body": "Updated body text",
                "body_format": "markdown",
                "status": "draft",
            }, editor_user.id)
            assert result["success"] is True
            versions = (
                ContentVersion.query
                .filter_by(content_id=sample_content.id)
                .order_by(ContentVersion.version_number.asc())
                .all()
            )
            assert len(versions) >= 2
            # Earliest version contains the original body
            assert versions[0].body == old_body

    def test_update_increments_version_number(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
        """current_version is incremented on each save."""
        with app.app_context():
            original_version = sample_content.current_version
            content_service.save_content(sample_content.id, {
                "title": "Updated Title",
                "content_type": "article",
                "body": "Updated body",
                "body_format": "markdown",
                "status": "draft",
            }, editor_user.id)
            content = Content.query.get(sample_content.id)
            assert content.current_version == original_version + 1


# ── test_workflow ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestWorkflow:
    def test_submit_for_review(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Status changes from draft to in_review."""
        with app.app_context():
            result = content_service.submit_for_review(sample_content.id, editor_user.id)
            assert result["success"] is True
            content = Content.query.get(sample_content.id)
            assert content.status == "in_review"

    def test_submit_non_draft_fails(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Cannot submit already in-review content."""
        with app.app_context():
            sample_content.status = "in_review"
            _db.session.commit()
            result = content_service.submit_for_review(sample_content.id, editor_user.id)
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert "draft" in result["reason"].lower()

    def test_submit_wrong_owner_fails(self, app, db, sample_users, sample_content):
        """Non-owner non-admin cannot submit for review."""
        with app.app_context():
            result = content_service.submit_for_review(
                sample_content.id, sample_users["customer"].id
            )
            assert result["success"] is False

    def test_publish_creates_version(self, app, db, sample_users, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Publishing creates a new ContentVersion with status 'published'."""
        with app.app_context():
            sample_content.status = "in_review"
            _db.session.commit()
            result = content_service.publish_content(sample_content.id, sample_users["admin"].id)
            assert result["success"] is True
            content = Content.query.get(sample_content.id)
            assert content.status == "published"
            assert content.published_at is not None
            versions = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(content_id=sample_content.id).all()
            assert any(v.status == "published" for v in versions)

    def test_publish_requires_admin(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Non-admin cannot publish."""
        with app.app_context():
            sample_content.status = "in_review"
            _db.session.commit()
            result = content_service.publish_content(sample_content.id, editor_user.id)
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert "admin" in result["reason"].lower()

    def test_publish_requires_in_review_status(self, app, db, sample_users, sample_content):
        """Publishing a draft (not in_review) is rejected."""
        with app.app_context():
            result = content_service.publish_content(
                sample_content.id, sample_users["admin"].id
            )
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert "review" in result["reason"].lower()

    def test_reject_requires_note(self, app, db, sample_users, sample_content):
        """Rejection without note fails."""
        with app.app_context():
            sample_content.status = "in_review"
            _db.session.commit()
            result = content_service.reject_content(
                sample_content.id, sample_users["admin"].id, ""
            )
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert "note" in result["reason"].lower()

    def test_reject_short_note_fails(self, app, db, sample_users, sample_content):
        """Rejection note shorter than 5 chars is rejected."""
        with app.app_context():
            sample_content.status = "in_review"
            _db.session.commit()
            result = content_service.reject_content(
                sample_content.id, sample_users["admin"].id, "bad"
            )
            assert result["success"] is False

    def test_reject_returns_to_draft(self, app, db, sample_users, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Rejected content status reverts to draft."""
        with app.app_context():
            sample_content.status = "in_review"
            _db.session.commit()
            result = content_service.reject_content(
                sample_content.id,
                sample_users["admin"].id,
                "Needs more detail please.",
            )
            assert result["success"] is True
            content = Content.query.get(sample_content.id)
            assert content.status == "draft"

    def test_reject_saves_note_in_version(self, app, db, sample_users, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Rejection note is stored in the ContentVersion snapshot."""
        with app.app_context():
            sample_content.status = "in_review"
            _db.session.commit()
            note = "Please revise the introduction."
            content_service.reject_content(
                sample_content.id, sample_users["admin"].id, note
            )
            versions = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(
                content_id=sample_content.id, status="rejected"
            ).all()
            assert len(versions) == 1
            assert versions[0].change_note == note


# ── test_rollback ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestRollback:
    def test_rollback_restores_body(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Rollback restores body to selected version."""
        with app.app_context():
            original_body = sample_content.body
            content_service.save_content(sample_content.id, {
                "title": sample_content.title,
                "content_type": "article",
                "body": "Completely different body",
                "body_format": "markdown",
                "status": "draft",
            }, editor_user.id)
            assert Content.query.get(sample_content.id).body == "Completely different body"

            version1 = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(
                content_id=sample_content.id, version_number=1
            ).first()
            result = content_service.rollback_to_version(
                sample_content.id, version1.id, editor_user.id
            )
            assert result["success"] is True
            restored = Content.query.get(sample_content.id)
            assert restored.body == original_body
            assert restored.status == "draft"

    def test_rollback_sets_draft(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Rollback always resets status to draft regardless of prior status."""
        with app.app_context():
            sample_content.status = "published"
            _db.session.commit()
            version1 = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(
                content_id=sample_content.id, version_number=1
            ).first()
            content_service.rollback_to_version(
                sample_content.id, version1.id, editor_user.id
            )
            assert Content.query.get(sample_content.id).status == "draft"

    def test_rollback_wrong_content_fails(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Rolling back with a version_id from a different content fails."""
        with app.app_context():
            # Create a second piece of content with its own version
            result2 = content_service.save_content(None, {
                "title": "Other Article",
                "content_type": "article",
                "body": "Other body",
                "body_format": "markdown",
                "status": "draft",
            }, editor_user.id)
            other_version = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(
                content_id=result2["content_id"]
            ).first()
            # Try to apply other_version to sample_content
            result = content_service.rollback_to_version(
                sample_content.id, other_version.id, editor_user.id
            )
            assert result["success"] is False


# ── test_content_filters ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestContentFilters:
    def test_content_filter_keyword(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_filter):
        """Content containing a filtered keyword is blocked."""
        with app.app_context():
            result = content_service.save_content(None, {
                "title": "Article with badword inside",
                "content_type": "article",
                "body": "Normal body",
                "body_format": "markdown",
                "status": "draft",
            }, editor_user.id)
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert len(result["violations"]) > 0

    def test_filter_in_body_blocked(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_filter):
        """Filtered keyword in body is caught."""
        with app.app_context():
            result = content_service.save_content(None, {
                "title": "Clean Title",
                "content_type": "article",
                "body": "This body has badword in it.",
                "body_format": "markdown",
                "status": "draft",
            }, editor_user.id)
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert any(v["match"].lower() == "badword" for v in result["violations"])

    def test_content_filter_regex(self, app, db):
        """Regex filter catches matching patterns."""
        with app.app_context():
            regex_filter = ContentFilter(
                pattern=r"\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b",
                filter_type="regex",
                is_active=True,
            )
            _db.session.add(regex_filter)
            _db.session.commit()
            result = filter_content("My SSN is 123-45-6789 please help")
            assert result["passed"] is False
            assert len(result["violations"]) == 1
            assert result["violations"][0]["filter_type"] == "regex"

    def test_content_filter_inactive_ignored(self, app, db):
        """Inactive filters are not applied."""
        with app.app_context():
            f = ContentFilter(pattern="testword", filter_type="keyword", is_active=False)
            _db.session.add(f)
            _db.session.commit()
            result = filter_content("This has testword in it")
            assert result["passed"] is True
            assert result["violations"] == []

    def test_filter_empty_text_passes(self, app, db, sample_filter):
        """Empty or None text always passes filtering."""
        with app.app_context():
            assert filter_content("") == {"passed": True, "violations": []}
            assert filter_content(None) == {"passed": True, "violations": []}

    def test_filter_case_insensitive(self, app, db):
        """Keyword filter is case-insensitive."""
        with app.app_context():
            f = ContentFilter(pattern="Spam", filter_type="keyword", is_active=True)
            _db.session.add(f)
            _db.session.commit()
            result = filter_content("This contains SPAM in uppercase")
            assert result["passed"] is False

    def test_invalid_regex_skipped(self, app, db):
        """A filter with an invalid regex pattern is skipped without crashing."""
        with app.app_context():
            bad = ContentFilter(pattern="[invalid(", filter_type="regex", is_active=True)
            _db.session.add(bad)
            _db.session.commit()
            result = filter_content("Some safe text here")
            # Should not raise; bad pattern is warned and skipped
            assert isinstance(result, dict)
            assert "passed" in result


# ── test_file_service ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestFileService:
    def test_file_upload_validates_type(self, app, db, sample_content):
        """Executable file is rejected."""
        with app.app_context():
            bad_file = FileStorage(stream=BytesIO(b"fake data"), filename="hack.exe")
            result = file_service.upload_file(bad_file, sample_content.id)
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert ".exe" in result["reason"]

    def test_file_upload_validates_size(self, app, db, sample_content):
        """File over 10 MB is rejected."""
        with app.app_context():
            large_data = b"x" * (11 * 1024 * 1024)
            big_file = FileStorage(stream=BytesIO(large_data), filename="big.jpg")
            result = file_service.upload_file(big_file, sample_content.id)
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert "10 MB" in result["reason"]

    def test_duplicate_file_rejected(self, app, db, sample_content):
        """Same file uploaded twice is rejected by fingerprint."""
        with app.app_context():
            data = b"unique test content for dedup"
            file1 = FileStorage(stream=BytesIO(data), filename="photo1.jpg")
            result1 = file_service.upload_file(file1, sample_content.id)
            assert result1["success"] is True

            file2 = FileStorage(stream=BytesIO(data), filename="photo2.jpg")
            result2 = file_service.upload_file(file2, sample_content.id)
            assert result2["success"] is False
            assert "already been uploaded" in result2["reason"]

    def test_file_upload_no_filename(self, app, db, sample_content):
        """FileStorage with no filename is rejected."""
        with app.app_context():
            empty = FileStorage(stream=BytesIO(b""), filename="")
            result = file_service.upload_file(empty, sample_content.id)
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert "No file" in result["reason"]

    def test_file_upload_success_returns_attachment_id(self, app, db, sample_content):
        """Successful upload returns attachment_id and creates DB record."""
        with app.app_context():
            data = b"valid jpg content for upload test"
            f = FileStorage(stream=BytesIO(data), filename="image.jpg")
            result = file_service.upload_file(f, sample_content.id)
            assert result["success"] is True
            assert "attachment_id" in result
            att = ContentAttachment.query.get(result["attachment_id"])
            assert att is not None
            assert att.content_id == sample_content.id
            assert att.original_filename == "image.jpg"

    def test_delete_file_removes_record(self, app, db, sample_content):
        """delete_file removes the ContentAttachment record."""
        with app.app_context():
            data = b"content to delete later"
            f = FileStorage(stream=BytesIO(data), filename="todelete.jpg")
            upload = file_service.upload_file(f, sample_content.id)
            assert upload["success"] is True
            att_id = upload["attachment_id"]

            result = file_service.delete_file(att_id)
            assert result["success"] is True
            assert ContentAttachment.query.get(att_id) is None

    def test_delete_nonexistent_file(self, app, db):
        """Deleting a non-existent attachment returns a clear error."""
        with app.app_context():
            result = file_service.delete_file(99999)
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert "not found" in result["reason"].lower()


# ── test_markdown_preview ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestMarkdownPreview:
    def test_markdown_preview(self, app):
        """Markdown is converted to HTML."""
        with app.app_context():
            html = content_service.preview_markdown("# Hello\n**Bold text**")
            assert "<h1>" in html or "<h1 " in html
            assert "<strong>" in html

    def test_empty_preview(self, app):
        """Empty input returns empty string."""
        with app.app_context():
            html = content_service.preview_markdown("")
            assert html == ""

    def test_preview_code_block(self, app):
        """Code fences are rendered."""
        with app.app_context():
            html = content_service.preview_markdown("```python\nprint('hi')\n```")
            assert "<code" in html


# ── test_dashboard ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestEditorDashboard:
    def test_editor_dashboard_editors_see_own(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Editors see only their own content."""
        with app.app_context():
            items = content_service.get_editor_dashboard(editor_user.id, "editor")
            assert len(items) >= 1
            assert all(item["author_name"] == editor_user.username for item in items)

    def test_admin_dashboard_sees_all(self, app, db, sample_users, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Admins see all content regardless of author."""
        with app.app_context():
            items = content_service.get_editor_dashboard(
                sample_users["admin"].id, "admin"
            )
            assert len(items) >= 1

    def test_dashboard_status_color(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Status color field is present and correct for draft content."""
        with app.app_context():
            items = content_service.get_editor_dashboard(editor_user.id, "editor")
            draft_item = next(
                (i for i in items if i["id"] == sample_content.id), None
            )
            assert draft_item is not None
            assert draft_item["status_color"] == "gray"

    def test_published_content_visible_in_browse(self, app, db, sample_users, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Published content appears in get_published_content."""
        with app.app_context():
            # Publish via workflow
            sample_content.status = "in_review"
            _db.session.commit()
            content_service.publish_content(sample_content.id, sample_users["admin"].id)
            result = content_service.get_published_content()
            ids = [item["id"] for item in result["items"]]
            assert sample_content.id in ids

    def test_draft_not_in_published(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Draft content does not appear in public browse."""
        with app.app_context():
            result = content_service.get_published_content()
            ids = [item["id"] for item in result["items"]]
            assert sample_content.id not in ids

    def test_tags_serialised_correctly(self, app, db, editor_user):
        """Tags provided as a comma-separated string are stored and returned as list."""
        with app.app_context():
            result = content_service.save_content(None, {
                "title": "Tagged Article",
                "content_type": "article",
                "body": "Body",
                "body_format": "markdown",
                "status": "draft",
                "tags": "yoga, wellness, beginner",
            }, editor_user.id)
            import json
            content = Content.query.get(result["content_id"])
            tags = json.loads(content.tags)
            assert "yoga" in tags
            assert "wellness" in tags
            assert "beginner" in tags
"}

                ContentVersion.query
                .filter_by(content_id=sample_content.id)
                .order_by(ContentVersion.version_number.asc())
                .all()
            )
            assert len(versions) >= 2
            # Earliest version contains the original body
            assert versions[0].body == old_body

    def test_update_increments_version_number(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
        """current_version is incremented on each save."""
        with app.app_context():
            original_version = sample_content.current_version
            content_service.save_content(sample_content.id, {
                "title": "Updated Title",
                "content_type": "article",
                "body": "Updated body",
                "body_format": "markdown",
                "status": "draft",
            }, editor_user.id)
            content = Content.query.get(sample_content.id)
            assert content.current_version == original_version + 1

class TestWorkflow:
    def test_submit_for_review(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Status changes from draft to in_review."""
        with app.app_context():
            result = content_service.submit_for_review(sample_content.id, editor_user.id)
            assert result["success"] is True
            content = Content.query.get(sample_content.id)
            assert content.status == "in_review"

    def test_submit_non_draft_fails(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Cannot submit already in-review content."""
        with app.app_context():
            sample_content.status = "in_review"
            _db.session.commit()
            result = content_service.submit_for_review(sample_content.id, editor_user.id)
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert "draft" in result["reason"].lower()

    def test_submit_wrong_owner_fails(self, app, db, sample_users, sample_content):
        """Non-owner non-admin cannot submit for review."""
        with app.app_context():
            result = content_service.submit_for_review(
                sample_content.id, sample_users["customer"].id
            )
            assert result["success"] is False

    def test_publish_creates_version(self, app, db, sample_users, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Publishing creates a new ContentVersion with status 'published'."""
        with app.app_context():
            sample_content.status = "in_review"
            _db.session.commit()
            result = content_service.publish_content(sample_content.id, sample_users["admin"].id)
            assert result["success"] is True
            content = Content.query.get(sample_content.id)
            assert content.status == "published"
            assert content.published_at is not None
            versions = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(content_id=sample_content.id).all()
            assert any(v.status == "published" for v in versions)

    def test_publish_requires_admin(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Non-admin cannot publish."""
        with app.app_context():
            sample_content.status = "in_review"
            _db.session.commit()
            result = content_service.publish_content(sample_content.id, editor_user.id)
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert "admin" in result["reason"].lower()

    def test_publish_requires_in_review_status(self, app, db, sample_users, sample_content):
        """Publishing a draft (not in_review) is rejected."""
        with app.app_context():
            result = content_service.publish_content(
                sample_content.id, sample_users["admin"].id
            )
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert "review" in result["reason"].lower()

    def test_reject_requires_note(self, app, db, sample_users, sample_content):
        """Rejection without note fails."""
        with app.app_context():
            sample_content.status = "in_review"
            _db.session.commit()
            result = content_service.reject_content(
                sample_content.id, sample_users["admin"].id, ""
            )
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert "note" in result["reason"].lower()

    def test_reject_short_note_fails(self, app, db, sample_users, sample_content):
        """Rejection note shorter than 5 chars is rejected."""
        with app.app_context():
            sample_content.status = "in_review"
            _db.session.commit()
            result = content_service.reject_content(
                sample_content.id, sample_users["admin"].id, "bad"
            )
            assert result["success"] is False

    def test_reject_returns_to_draft(self, app, db, sample_users, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Rejected content status reverts to draft."""
        with app.app_context():
            sample_content.status = "in_review"
            _db.session.commit()
            result = content_service.reject_content(
                sample_content.id,
                sample_users["admin"].id,
                "Needs more detail please.",
            )
            assert result["success"] is True
            content = Content.query.get(sample_content.id)
            assert content.status == "draft"

    def test_reject_saves_note_in_version(self, app, db, sample_users, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Rejection note is stored in the ContentVersion snapshot."""
        with app.app_context():
            sample_content.status = "in_review"
            _db.session.commit()
            note = "Please revise the introduction."
            content_service.reject_content(
                sample_content.id, sample_users["admin"].id, note
            )
            versions = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(
                content_id=sample_content.id, status="rejected"
            ).all()
            assert len(versions) == 1
            assert versions[0].change_note == note


# ── test_rollback ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestRollback:
    def test_rollback_restores_body(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Rollback restores body to selected version."""
        with app.app_context():
            original_body = sample_content.body
            content_service.save_content(sample_content.id, {
                "title": sample_content.title,
                "content_type": "article",
                "body": "Completely different body",
                "body_format": "markdown",
                "status": "draft",
            }, editor_user.id)
            assert Content.query.get(sample_content.id).body == "Completely different body"

            version1 = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(
                content_id=sample_content.id, version_number=1
            ).first()
            result = content_service.rollback_to_version(
                sample_content.id, version1.id, editor_user.id
            )
            assert result["success"] is True
            restored = Content.query.get(sample_content.id)
            assert restored.body == original_body
            assert restored.status == "draft"

    def test_rollback_sets_draft(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Rollback always resets status to draft regardless of prior status."""
        with app.app_context():
            sample_content.status = "published"
            _db.session.commit()
            version1 = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(
                content_id=sample_content.id, version_number=1
            ).first()
            content_service.rollback_to_version(
                sample_content.id, version1.id, editor_user.id
            )
            assert Content.query.get(sample_content.id).status == "draft"

    def test_rollback_wrong_content_fails(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_content):
        """Rolling back with a version_id from a different content fails."""
        with app.app_context():
            # Create a second piece of content with its own version
            result2 = content_service.save_content(None, {
                "title": "Other Article",
                "content_type": "article",
                "body": "Other body",
                "body_format": "markdown",
                "status": "draft",
            }, editor_user.id)
            other_version = ContentVersion.query.filter_by(
                content_id=result2["content_id"]
            ).first()
            # Try to apply other_version to sample_content
            result = content_service.rollback_to_version(
                sample_content.id, other_version.id, editor_user.id
            )
            assert result["success"] is False


# ── test_content_filters ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────

class TestContentFilters:
    def test_content_filter_keyword(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_filter):
        """Content containing a filtered keyword is blocked."""
        with app.app_context():
            result = content_service.save_content(None, {
                "title": "Article with badword inside",
                "content_type": "article",
                "body": "Normal body",
                "body_format": "markdown",
                "status": "draft",
            }, editor_user.id)
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert len(result["violations"]) > 0

    def test_filter_in_body_blocked(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_filter):
        """Filtered keyword in body is caught."""
        with app.app_context():
            result = content_service.save_content(None, {
                "title": "Clean Title",
                "content_type": "article",
                "body": "This body has badword in it.",
                "body_format": "markdown",
                "status": "draft",
            }, editor_user.id)
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert any(v["match"].lower() == "badword" for v in result["violations"])

    def test_content_filter_regex(self, app, db):
        """Regex filter catches matching patterns."""
        with app.app_context():
            regex_filter = ContentFilter(
                pattern=r"\b\d{3}-\d{2}-\d{4}\b",
                filter_type="regex",
                is_active=True,
            )
            _db.session.add(regex_filter)
            _db.session.commit()
            result = filter_content("My SSN is 123-45-6789 please help")
            assert result["passed"] is False
            assert len(result["violations"]) == 1
            assert result["violations"][0]["filter_type"] == "regex"

    def test_content_filter_inactive_ignored(self, app, db):
        """Inactive filters are not applied."""
        with app.app_context():
            f = ContentFilter(pattern="testword", filter_type="keyword", is_active=False)
            _db.session.add(f)
            _db.session.commit()
            result = filter_content("This has testword in it")
            assert result["passed"] is True
            assert result["violations"] == []

    def test_filter_empty_text_passes(self, app, db, sample_filter):
        """Empty or None text always passes filtering."""
        with app.app_context():
            assert filter_content("") == {"passed": True, "violations": []}
            assert filter_content(None) == {"passed": True, "violations": []}

    def test_filter_case_insensitive(self, app, db):
        """Keyword filter is case-insensitive."""
        with app.app_context():
            f = ContentFilter(pattern="Spam", filter_type="keyword", is_active=True)
            _db.session.add(f)
            _db.session.commit()
            result = filter_content("This contains SPAM in uppercase")
            assert result["passed"] is False

    def test_invalid_regex_skipped(self, app, db):
        """A filter with an invalid regex pattern is skipped without crashing."""
        with app.app_context():
            bad = ContentFilter(pattern="[invalid(", filter_type="regex", is_active=True)
            _db.session.add(bad)
            _db.session.commit()
            result = filter_content("Some safe text here")
            # Should not raise; bad pattern is warned and skipped
            assert isinstance(result, dict)
            assert "passed" in result

class TestFileService:
    def test_file_upload_validates_type(self, app, db, sample_content):
        """Executable file is rejected."""
        with app.app_context():
            bad_file = FileStorage(stream=BytesIO(b"fake data"), filename="hack.exe")
            result = file_service.upload_file(bad_file, sample_content.id)
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert ".exe" in result["reason"]

    def test_file_upload_validates_size(self, app, db, sample_content):
        """File over 10 MB is rejected."""
        with app.app_context():
            large_data = b"x" * (11 * 1024 * 1024)
            big_file = FileStorage(stream=BytesIO(large_data), filename="big.jpg")
            result = file_service.upload_file(big_file, sample_content.id)
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert "10 MB" in result["reason"]

    def test_duplicate_file_rejected(self, app, db, sample_content):
        """Same file uploaded twice is rejected by fingerprint."""
        with app.app_context():
            data = b"unique test content for dedup"
            file1 = FileStorage(stream=BytesIO(data), filename="photo1.jpg")
            result1 = file_service.upload_file(file1, sample_content.id)
            assert result1["success"] is True

            file2 = FileStorage(stream=BytesIO(data), filename="photo2.jpg")
            result2 = file_service.upload_file(file2, sample_content.id)
            assert result2["success"] is False
            assert "already been uploaded" in result2["reason"]

    def test_file_upload_no_filename(self, app, db, sample_content):
        """FileStorage with no filename is rejected."""
        with app.app_context():
            empty = FileStorage(stream=BytesIO(b""), filename="")
            result = file_service.upload_file(empty, sample_content.id)
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert "No file" in result["reason"]

    def test_file_upload_success_returns_attachment_id(self, app, db, sample_content):
        """Successful upload returns attachment_id and creates DB record."""
        with app.app_context():
            data = b"valid jpg content for upload test"
            f = FileStorage(stream=BytesIO(data), filename="image.jpg")
            result = file_service.upload_file(f, sample_content.id)
            assert result["success"] is True
            assert "attachment_id" in result
            att = ContentAttachment.query.get(result["attachment_id"])
            assert att is not None
            assert att.content_id == sample_content.id
            assert att.original_filename == "image.jpg"

    def test_delete_file_removes_record(self, app, db, sample_content):
        """delete_file removes the ContentAttachment record."""
        with app.app_context():
            data = b"content to delete later"
            f = FileStorage(stream=BytesIO(data), filename="todelete.jpg")
            upload = file_service.upload_file(f, sample_content.id)
            assert upload["success"] is True
            att_id = upload["attachment_id"]

            result = file_service.delete_file(att_id)
            assert result["success"] is True
            assert ContentAttachment.query.get(att_id) is None

    def test_delete_nonexistent_file(self, app, db):
        """Deleting a non-existent attachment returns a clear error."""
        with app.app_context():
            result = file_service.delete_file(99999)
            assert result["success"] is False
            assert "not found" in result["reason"].lower()

class TestEditorDashboard:

            )

    def test_dashboard_status_color(self, app, db, editor_user, sample_content):

        with app.app_context():

                (i for i in items if i["id"] == sample_content.id), None
            )

    def test_tags_serialised_correctly(self, app, db, editor_user):
